the dance number with the mega-bar is peak Y2K public television energy.
it's so big you list it as a dependent on your taxes.

you get newborn-scale caramel real estate for grocery-aisle money.

middles_generic: consumer reporting at this point is just group therapy with receipts.
the product is fine; the branding is doing interpretive dance.
I wanted reliability and got a personality instead.
it's the year 2000 and we still can't normalize honest manuals.
support was polite and useless — peak professionalism.
I'll keep using it until the warranty ghosts me.
the price-to-annoyance ratio is acceptable.
works for me but I also still use a paper Rolodex.
your mileage may vary unless you drive a literal mile.
I feel seen, but mostly in a compliance way.
shipping arrived on 'sim-time soon' which is its own timezone.
the box had more integrity than the online FAQ.
I bought it for the specs; I stayed for the absurd warranty card.
setup was 'plug and pray' with a side of optimism.
returns process is a novella; the product is a short story.
quality is 'good enough for government work' — I mean that kindly.
the manual contradicts itself; I respect the chaos.
customer service escalated me to hold music tier three.
it does the job until the job evolves without telling anyone.
I compared three reviews and all three were written by the same mood.
unboxing felt like opening a time capsule from a confident decade.
the accessory bundle is a personality test I failed.
I expected mediocrity; I received competent mediocrity — win.
the forum flamewar had better specs than the product page.
it works on my machine, my machine being hope.
delivery driver was the highlight of the transaction.
the product matches the photo if you squint and believe.
I'm keeping it because returning it requires a notary.
stars are a social construct; this is three and a half vibes.
the invoice was clearer than the quick-start guide.
I read the fine print; I'm emotionally recovering.
it outlasted my interest, which says something.
the brand loyalty program is a spreadsheet and a dream.
I bought it on impulse; impulse has good taste this week.
noise level is acceptable if you enjoy white noise and mild regret.
the redesign fixed nothing I cared about and everything I didn't.
I'm still using Gen1 because patches are scary.
compatibility matrix looks like a family tree drawn in crayon.
the rebate form is a loyalty test; I folded early.
overall: would recommend to enemies and friends alike.
